provenance: Hand-derived golden fixture for compute_pbo (reduced-N CSCV, Bailey & López de Prado 2014). All values computed independently from first principles — NO producer (autotuner/math_engine) output used anywhere in this file. Method: for each of C(S,S/2)=C(8,4)=70 IS/OOS splits, the IS-best config is found by mean CRRA-EU on its IS block dates, then its 1-based rank among K configs by mean CRRA-EU on OOS block dates, lambda = ln((rank-0.5)/K - ln(1-(rank-0.5)/K)) = ln((rank-0.5) / (K - (rank-0.5))). PBO = fraction of 70 combos with lambda <= 0 (i.e. OOS rank <= K/2). Reference: Bailey & López de Prado 2014, 'The Probability of Backtest Overfitting', Journal of Computational Finance 20(4), DOI 10.21314/JCF.2014.005.

constants.PBO_REJECT_THRESHOLD_comment: derived — random selection implies PBO=0.5; >0.5 is worse than random; Bailey&LdP 2014

all_configs_dominate_oos.description: K=4 synthetic configs, S=4 blocks, 4 IS/4 OOS splits (C(4,2)=6). Config 0 has uniformly higher IS returns AND uniformly higher OOS returns across all blocks — the IS-best is always best OOS too, so every combo has rank_c=K (best rank, OOS rank 1-based descending) → lambda>0 → PBO=0.0.
all_configs_dominate_oos.note: We use S=4, K=4 here to make the hand-derivation tractable; the production constants are S=8/K=20 but the mathematical contract (lambda formula, PBO fraction) is identical.
all_configs_dominate_oos.configs_date_returns_comment: Each config is a dict mapping date->return (decimal fraction). Config 0 dominates: it has +1% on all dates. Configs 1-3 have 0%, -0.5%, -1% respectively. IS-best will always be config 0; its OOS rank across all combos will be 1st (best among K). Expected PBO=0.0.

expected.pbo_comment: Config 0 dominates IS and OOS in every partition. rank_c=K=4 for all combos (IS-best is also OOS-best). omega_bar=(4-0.5)/4=0.875, lambda=ln(0.875/0.125)=1.9459>0. All 6 combos have lambda>0 → PBO=0/6=0.0.

is_best_always_worst_oos.description: Block-singleton design: K=4 configs, S=4 blocks (6 dates each = 24 dates total). Config k has positive returns ONLY on block k; absent from all other blocks (scored as empty = 0.0 CRRA-EU). Strictly decreasing magnitudes (r0=10%>r1=8%>r2=6%>r3=4%) break all IS-score ties. For every IS/OOS combination the IS-best config has zero OOS returns (its block is always in IS, not OOS), making it the lowest-scoring or joint-lowest OOS config in every combo. PBO = 6/6 = 1.0.
is_best_always_worst_oos.provenance_note: Hand-derived from first principles. Original alternating-sign design was WRONG (PBO=0.0 — CRRA risk aversion penalises the ±5% alternating config more than the flat-0% config, so the alternating config never wins IS). This block-singleton design was verified by enumerating all 6 combos by hand and confirmed with compute_pbo returning 1.0. See team-pbo-gate Revise log 2026-06-01.
is_best_always_worst_oos.configs_date_returns_comment: Config k has return r_k ONLY on block k dates; absent from all other dates (treated as empty = 0 CRRA-EU). r0=0.10, r1=0.08, r2=0.06, r3=0.04 (strictly decreasing to break IS ties). CRRA-EU with gamma=2: U(W)=1-1/W. U(1.10)=0.09091, U(1.08)=0.07407, U(1.06)=0.05660, U(1.04)=0.03846. IS score for cfg k in a 2-block IS set = U(1+r_k)/2 (6 active dates + 6 zero dates, 12 total). OOS score for cfg k when its block is in IS = 0.0 (no dates in OOS blocks).

hand_derivation.summary: All 6 combos: IS-best has OOS score=0.0 (its block is always in IS, not OOS). The other IS config also scores 0.0 OOS. rank_c=2 for all combos. omega_bar=1.5/4=0.375, lambda=ln(0.375/0.625)=-0.5108<0. PBO=6/6=1.0.

expected.pbo_comment: IS-best always has OOS score=0.0 (its block is in IS, not OOS). The other IS-side config also has OOS=0.0 (its block is also in IS). rank_c=2 (tied at bottom with the other IS config), omega_bar=(2-0.5)/4=0.375, lambda=ln(0.375/0.625)=-0.5108<0. All 6 combos counted → PBO=6/6=1.0.

lambda_formula_pin.description: Direct verification of the lambda formula for specific (rank_c, K) pairs. lambda_c = ln((rank_c - 0.5) / (K - (rank_c - 0.5))). These are hand-computed values that the implementation must reproduce exactly.
lambda_formula_pin.note: For K=4: rank 1 (worst OOS) → omega_bar=0.125, lambda=ln(0.125/0.875)=-1.9459101490553135. rank 2 → omega_bar=0.375, lambda=ln(0.375/0.625)=-0.5108256237659907. rank 3 → omega_bar=0.625, lambda=ln(0.625/0.375)=0.5108256237659907. rank 4 (best OOS) → omega_bar=0.875, lambda=ln(0.875/0.125)=1.9459101490553132. All values computed via Python math.log(omega/(1-omega)).

boundary_case.description: At exactly K/2 = rank_c, lambda must equal exactly 0.0 (boundary). With K=4, rank=2 gives lambda<0, rank=3 gives lambda>0. PBO=0.5 boundary occurs when exactly half the combos have rank<=K/2.
boundary_case.comment: lambda=0 only when omega_bar=0.5, i.e., rank_c-0.5=K/2, i.e., rank_c=K/2+0.5. For integer K this never hits exactly — the PBO=0.5 boundary is defined by the FRACTION of combos with lambda<=0.
